indicator_text: 2015 index of lobbying regulation (composite of all 19 components)
scoring_rule: each of the above 19 categories was given a 1 if the state included the provision and a 0 if it did not, and the categories were then summed to create an index assessing the level of lobbying regulation in each state.
source_quote: each of the above 19 categories was given a 1 if the state included the provision and a 0 if it did not, and the categories were then summed to create an index [papers/text/Newmark_2017__lobbying_regulation_revisited.txt:555-558]
notes: Composite. Decomposes into the 3 sub-aggregates (def.section_total, prohib.section_total, disclosure.section_total) and 19 atomic items below. Observed range 7-19 across 50 states; mean 12.96, SD 2.63; Cronbach's alpha 0.67. Newmark's 2015 measure (paper's headline contribution).

indicator_text: 2015 definitions sub-score (7 items)
section_or_category: Definitions of lobbyists and registration requirements
scoring_rule: Sum of seven 1/0 items in the Definitions category.
source_quote: Definitions include whether the following necessitate registering as a lobbyist: legislative lobbying, administrative agency lobbying, elective officials as lobbyists, public employees as lobbyists, and whether there are compensation, expenditure, and time standards. [papers/text/Newmark_2017__lobbying_regulation_revisited.txt:515-518]
notes: Composite (sub-aggregate). Decomposes into the 7 def.* items below. Reported per state in Table 2 column "2015 definitions" (range 1-7). Newmark explicitly enumerates the 7 components in this category. Lineage: BoS-derived (Newmark notes "categories listed above are used in previous studies derived from the BOS").

indicator_text: Legislative lobbying necessitates registering as a lobbyist
section_or_category: Definitions of lobbyists and registration requirements
scoring_rule: Each of these is coded 1 if the state includes the provision in its definition of a lobbyist and 0 otherwise.
source_quote: Definitions include whether the following necessitate registering as a lobbyist: legislative lobbying, administrative agency lobbying [papers/text/Newmark_2017__lobbying_regulation_revisited.txt:515-516]
notes: BoS-derived (Newmark 2017 reproduces BoS categories via statute review). Newmark notes "All 50 states require registration for lobbying the legislature" (line 518), so this item has no variation in 2015 — and it is excluded from the factor analysis for that reason (Table 3 footnote: "Legislative lobbying ... excluded because there is no variation").

indicator_text: Administrative agency lobbying necessitates registering as a lobbyist
section_or_category: Definitions of lobbyists and registration requirements
scoring_rule: Each of these is coded 1 if the state includes the provision in its definition of a lobbyist and 0 otherwise.
source_quote: administrative agency lobbying, elective officials as lobbyists, public employees as lobbyists [papers/text/Newmark_2017__lobbying_regulation_revisited.txt:516-517]
notes: BoS-derived. Newmark notes "most states require it for lobbying administrative agencies" (line 519). Loads on factor 1 (0.622) and factor 4 (-0.543) in factor analysis.

indicator_text: Elective officials are defined as lobbyists if engaged in lobbying-related behaviors
section_or_category: Definitions of lobbyists and registration requirements
scoring_rule: Each of these is coded 1 if the state includes the provision in its definition of a lobbyist and 0 otherwise.
source_quote: Some states require elected officials and public appointees to register if they engage in certain lobbying-related behaviors. [papers/text/Newmark_2017__lobbying_regulation_revisited.txt:519-521]
notes: BoS-derived. Note paper uses both "elective officials" (in enumeration) and "elected officials" (in commentary) — same item. Loads 0.790 on factor 4.

indicator_text: Public employees are defined as lobbyists if engaged in lobbying-related behaviors
section_or_category: Definitions of lobbyists and registration requirements
scoring_rule: Each of these is coded 1 if the state includes the provision in its definition of a lobbyist and 0 otherwise.
source_quote: public employees as lobbyists, and whether there are compensation, expenditure, and time standards [papers/text/Newmark_2017__lobbying_regulation_revisited.txt:517-518]
notes: BoS-derived. Loads 0.305 on factor 1 and 0.630 on factor 4.

indicator_text: Compensation standard for lobbyist status (state defines persons as lobbyists if they receive a certain amount of money in exchange for lobbying)
section_or_category: Definitions of lobbyists and registration requirements
scoring_rule: Each of these is coded 1 if the state includes the provision in its definition of a lobbyist and 0 otherwise.
source_quote: persons are defined as lobbyists if they receive a certain amount of money in exchange for lobbying (compensation standards) [papers/text/Newmark_2017__lobbying_regulation_revisited.txt:521-523]
notes: BoS-derived. Threshold dollar value not specified by Newmark — only the presence/absence of any compensation threshold in the definition is scored. Atomization: Newmark groups "compensation, expenditure, and time standards" in one enumeration but treats each as a separate component (Table 2 column shows up to 7 def points, which only reaches 7 if all three standards are separate items). Loads 0.781 on factor 1.

indicator_text: Expenditure standard for lobbyist status (state defines persons as lobbyists if they spend a certain amount of money in lobbying)
section_or_category: Definitions of lobbyists and registration requirements
scoring_rule: Each of these is coded 1 if the state includes the provision in its definition of a lobbyist and 0 otherwise.
source_quote: if they spend a certain amount of money in lobbying (expenditure standards) [papers/text/Newmark_2017__lobbying_regulation_revisited.txt:523-524]
notes: BoS-derived. Threshold dollar value not specified. Loads 0.563 on factor 1.

indicator_text: Time standard for lobbyist status (state defines persons as lobbyists if they devote a certain amount of time in lobbying)
section_or_category: Definitions of lobbyists and registration requirements
scoring_rule: Each of these is coded 1 if the state includes the provision in its definition of a lobbyist and 0 otherwise.
source_quote: if they devote a certain amount of time in their lobbying efforts (time standards). Each of these is coded 1 if the state includes the provision in its definition of a lobbyist and 0 otherwise. [papers/text/Newmark_2017__lobbying_regulation_revisited.txt:524-526]
notes: BoS-derived. Threshold time value not specified. Loads 0.493 on factor 1 and 0.444 on factor 4.

indicator_text: 2015 prohibited activities sub-score (5 items)
scoring_rule: Sum of five 1/0 items in the Prohibited activities category.
source_quote: Prohibited activities include: if lobbyists are prohibited from making campaign contributions at any time; if they are prohibited from making campaign contributions during the legislative sessions; solicitations by officials or employees for contributions or gifts; contingent compensation; and revolving door restrictions. [papers/text/Newmark_2017__lobbying_regulation_revisited.txt:528-532]
notes: Composite (sub-aggregate). Decomposes into the 5 prohib.* items below. Reported per state in Table 2 column "2015 prohibited activities" (range 0-5). Lineage: BoS-derived.

indicator_text: Lobbyists prohibited from making campaign contributions at any time
scoring_rule: each is coded 1/0 depending on if the state prohibits a given activity.
source_quote: if lobbyists are prohibited from making campaign contributions at any time [papers/text/Newmark_2017__lobbying_regulation_revisited.txt:529-530]
notes: BoS-derived. Loads 0.558 on factor 2.

indicator_text: Lobbyists prohibited from making campaign contributions during legislative sessions
scoring_rule: each is coded 1/0 depending on if the state prohibits a given activity.
source_quote: if they are prohibited from making campaign contributions during the legislative sessions [papers/text/Newmark_2017__lobbying_regulation_revisited.txt:530-531]
notes: BoS-derived. Loads 0.329 on factor 2.

indicator_text: Solicitation by officials or employees for contributions or gifts is prohibited
scoring_rule: each is coded 1/0 depending on if the state prohibits a given activity.
source_quote: solicitations by officials or employees for contributions or gifts [papers/text/Newmark_2017__lobbying_regulation_revisited.txt:531-532]
notes: BoS-derived. Note: this item restricts the *officials*' demand-side behavior, not the lobbyist's supply-side. Loads 0.652 on factor 3 and -0.346 on factor 1.

indicator_text: Contingent compensation is prohibited (lobbyists receiving pay only on achieving a lobbying-related objective)
scoring_rule: each is coded 1/0 depending on if the state prohibits a given activity.
source_quote: Contingent compensation refers to restrictions on lobbyists receiving pay only if they achieve some lobbying-related objective. [papers/text/Newmark_2017__lobbying_regulation_revisited.txt:533-535]
notes: BoS-derived. Loads 0.522 on factor 2.

indicator_text: Revolving door restrictions (lawmakers prohibited from becoming lobbyists without a waiting period)
scoring_rule: each is coded 1/0 depending on if the state prohibits a given activity.
source_quote: Revolving door laws prohibit lawmakers from becoming lobbyists without a waiting period before they can lobby. [papers/text/Newmark_2017__lobbying_regulation_revisited.txt:535-536]
notes: BoS-derived. Length of waiting period not coded — only presence/absence of any cooling-off requirement. Newmark notes elsewhere FL/IA require 2 years; TX/NE/VT require none (lines 134-136). Loads 0.534 on factor 2 and 0.345 on factor 4.

indicator_text: 2015 disclosure/reporting sub-score (7 items)
scoring_rule: Sum of seven 1/0 items in the Disclosure category.
source_quote: Registration and reporting/disclosure requirements, which include whether the following activities require reporting or disclosure: seeking to influence legislation or administrative action, expenditures benefitting public officials or employees, compensation received (broken down by employer), total compensations received, categories of expenditures, total expenditures toward lobbying, and contributions received from others for lobbying purposes. [papers/text/Newmark_2017__lobbying_regulation_revisited.txt:539-545]
notes: Composite (sub-aggregate). Decomposes into the 7 disclosure.* items below. Reported per state in Table 2 column "2015 disclosure/reporting" (range 2-7). 15 states had max score of 7. Lineage: BoS-derived.

indicator_text: Disclosure required: seeking to influence legislation or administrative action
scoring_rule: if the state requires that a lobbyist discloses that they sought to influence legislation or if they spent money in a way that benefited a public official, they were coded 1 for each category (0 if not).
source_quote: seeking to influence legislation or administrative action [papers/text/Newmark_2017__lobbying_regulation_revisited.txt:540-541]
notes: BoS-derived. Loads 0.766 on factor 1.

indicator_text: Disclosure required: expenditures benefiting public officials or employees
scoring_rule: if the state requires that a lobbyist discloses that they sought to influence legislation or if they spent money in a way that benefited a public official, they were coded 1 for each category (0 if not).
source_quote: expenditures benefitting public officials or employees [papers/text/Newmark_2017__lobbying_regulation_revisited.txt:541-542]
notes: BoS-derived. Excluded from factor analysis ("no variation in this category" per Table 3 footnote, line 1162-1163). Spelling in source: "benefitting" (with double t).

indicator_text: Disclosure required: compensation received broken down by employer
scoring_rule: Coded 1 if state requires this disclosure category, 0 if not.
source_quote: compensation received (broken down by employer) [papers/text/Newmark_2017__lobbying_regulation_revisited.txt:542-543]
notes: BoS-derived. Loads 0.744 on factor 2 and 0.331 on factor 1.

indicator_text: Disclosure required: total compensation received
scoring_rule: Coded 1 if state requires this disclosure category, 0 if not.
source_quote: total compensations received [papers/text/Newmark_2017__lobbying_regulation_revisited.txt:543]
notes: BoS-derived. Source uses plural "compensations" — same construct as singular "total compensation" used in Table 3. Loads 0.834 on factor 2.

indicator_text: Disclosure required: categories of expenditures
scoring_rule: Coded 1 if state requires this disclosure category, 0 if not.
source_quote: categories of expenditures [papers/text/Newmark_2017__lobbying_regulation_revisited.txt:543]
notes: BoS-derived. Loads 0.702 on factor 3.

indicator_text: Disclosure required: total expenditures toward lobbying
scoring_rule: Coded 1 if state requires this disclosure category, 0 if not.
source_quote: total expenditures toward lobbying [papers/text/Newmark_2017__lobbying_regulation_revisited.txt:543-544]
notes: BoS-derived. Loads 0.778 on factor 3.

indicator_text: Disclosure required: contributions received from others for lobbying purposes
scoring_rule: Coded 1 if state requires this disclosure category, 0 if not.
source_quote: contributions received from others for lobbying purposes [papers/text/Newmark_2017__lobbying_regulation_revisited.txt:547]
notes: BoS-derived. Loads 0.418 on factor 1 and 0.413 on factor 2.
